description_long: This bungalow features a low, wide profile with a prominent central gable and symmetrical rooflines, typical of early 20th-century suburban homes. The roof is steeply pitched with visible stepped layers, and the main entrance is recessed beneath a covered porch area. The structure appears to have a single story, with a compact footprint and a rectangular base. Window openings are visible on the front and rear facades, arranged in a balanced, symmetrical pattern. The exterior is rendered in a uniform, dark material, suggesting a solid masonry or concrete construction. The overall design is simple and functional, with minimal ornamentation and a focus on horizontal lines.
description: A single-story bungalow with a steeply pitched, symmetrical gable roof and a recessed front porch, rendered in a dark, solid material. The structure features balanced window openings and a compact, rectangular footprint.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central gable, recessed porch, and symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the roof and the covered porch extension.
description_view_2: Rear view with visible window openings and the continuation of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the solid wall and additional window placements.
